factors.data_availability: low — lupus nephritis biopsy is a clinical procedure, not routine biobanking; commercial availability is thin
factors.org_discoverability: medium — academic rheumatology centers publish but specimen re-access requires negotiation
factors.biomarker_specificity: high — ISN/RPS classification requires expert nephropathology and is not tracked in any biospecimen registry

expected_failure_modes: Lupus nephritis biopsy is a diagnostic clinical procedure performed at diagnosis, not routine biobanking — commercial biobank availability is structurally thin
ISN/RPS class III-V requires expert nephropathology review — pipeline must flag as unknown, not infer from diagnosis codes
ANA and anti-dsDNA are serological markers not tracked in tissue registries — pipeline must not hallucinate documented status
Pipeline may find AMP-LN consortium data but physical specimen re-access from that consortium is controlled and not equivalent to commercial procurement
With only 3 orgs and 102 donors in AminoChain, N >= 20 with ISN/RPS class documentation is unlikely to be confirmed without direct org inquiry
